title: soliva sessilis var. barclayana
intro: Soliva sessilis var. barclayana, also known as the small-flowered soliva or Barclay's soliva, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antimicrobial agent. The health benefits associated with Soliva sessilis var. barclayana include its potential to treat wounds, skin conditions, and infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with other medications.
